behaviors.description: A static prompt-template lint check rejects phase-specific instruction leakage copied from SKILL.md.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: static linting of prompt template text for leaked phase instructions; rejection decision is based on template content/rules. Hypothetical elsewhere: failures in harness interpolation, file loading, model behavior. MOCK-MISS: failure is in local lint logic, not module handoff. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: mocks do not hide the core failure because input is plain text. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration/System/Agentic rejected because behavior text says "static prompt-template lint check" (single local analysis step).
behaviors.test_description: Call the lint function with a template containing a verbatim phase instruction copied from SKILL.md and assert it is rejected with the expected violation type; include a near-miss control case to avoid overblocking.

behaviors.description: The fully assembled prompt produced by the real test harness (after interpolation/fixtures) still contains no phase-instruction leakage.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: prompt assembly through real harness path, including interpolation/fixtures, then leakage check on final assembled prompt. Hypothetical elsewhere: real HTTP/model invocation. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing only template pieces can miss leakage introduced at module boundaries during assembly. REAL-INFRA: no required real network/subprocess/OS primitive; in-process harness wiring is sufficient. MOCK-HIDE: mocking assembler could hide boundary bug; using real harness modules avoids that. LLM-DEP: none. MULTI-STEP: no agentic chain. Integration-brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess requirement, (b) no AI output dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Run the actual prompt-building pipeline with representative fixtures and interpolation values, then assert the final prompt string does not contain forbidden phase-instruction spans or normalized equivalents.

behaviors.description: The test invocation loads skill context from the plugin/skill files and validates behavior from that context rather than from hard-coded prompt rules.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: invocation path must read plugin/skill files and use that context in behavior validation; distinguishes file-sourced context from hard-coded prompt rules. Hypothetical elsewhere: model quality variability. MOCK-MISS: single-function tests can miss miswiring where loader output is ignored and hard-coded rules are used. REAL-INFRA: local filesystem access is enough and fits Integration; no subprocess/network requirement is explicit. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing loader risks always passing even if real loader is bypassed. LLM-DEP: not required by this specific behavior text (it targets context source wiring). MULTI-STEP: none. Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess behavior beyond normal file reads, (b) no required real AI output quality check, (c) no multi-agentic chain. Rejection gate: Agentic rejected because behavior explicitly checks source of context, not semantic quality of model output.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the real test invocation with plugin/skill files present and assert provenance signals (e.g., loaded section IDs/content fingerprints) are used in validation path; fail if hard-coded prompt rules can satisfy the check without file-loaded context.

behaviors.description: If the relevant SKILL.md section is removed or degraded, the behavioral test fails with the same user-style prompt.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: with identical user-style prompt, changing SKILL.md quality/content must cause behavioral failure. This is a sensitivity-to-skill-content outcome check, not just wiring. Hypothetical elsewhere: orchestration of multiple agent steps. MOCK-MISS: deterministic stubs can be scripted to pass/fail and may hide real model sensitivity failures. REAL-INFRA: may run through normal harness, but core boundary is model behavior dependency. LLM-DEP: yes, correctness depends on what real model produces from changed skill context; outcomes can vary across runs. MULTI-STEP: not explicitly multiple agentic stages in sequence. Rejection gate: Workflow rejected because behavior specifies a single user-style prompt comparison, not a chained multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run behavioral smoke test against baseline SKILL.md and against a degraded/removed target section using the same user-style prompt; require degraded variant to fail on structured criteria with retry/statistical guardrails to handle model variance.

behaviors.description: Raw model output is captured to a diagnostic artifact before any JSON parsing or extraction logic runs.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: ordering constraint across modules (model-response capture must happen before parser/extractor), plus artifact persistence. Hypothetical elsewhere: external API instability. MOCK-MISS: unit-testing parser alone misses orchestration-order bugs. REAL-INFRA: no mandatory real network/subprocess; can use stubbed model response while exercising real capture+parse pipeline and filesystem artifact write. MOCK-HIDE: mocking the capture layer itself could hide ordering defects; use real pipeline modules. LLM-DEP: none (content quality not under test). MULTI-STEP: none. Integration-brake: (a) no required real OS/network/subprocess semantics beyond standard file write, (b) no AI quality dependency, (c) no multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the response-processing pipeline with a controlled model-output payload and assert a raw-output artifact exists and contains the unparsed payload before parser/extractor stage executes (e.g., via event order markers or hooks).

behaviors.description: Assertions verify structured behavior (for example field placement or schema shape), not mere keyword presence that could come from prompt echoing.
behaviors.justification: Observed in this behavior: assertion semantics must enforce structure/schema, avoiding false passes from echoed keywords. Hypothetical elsewhere: harness invocation or model variability. MOCK-MISS: core failure is weak assertion logic; local assertion function/rules can catch it directly. REAL-INFRA: no OS/network/subprocess required. MOCK-HIDE: not if tests feed crafted outputs (echoed keywords with wrong structure). LLM-DEP: none required for minimum failure detection. MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection gate: Integration/Agentic rejected because behavior text focuses on assertion criteria design, which can be validated with synthetic outputs independent of full pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Feed assertion logic with adversarial fixtures: one output containing expected keywords but wrong schema/field placement, and one truly valid structured output; assert first fails and second passes.
